material: Silicon substrate with graphene (or graphitic carbon) thin film/flake

eliminated_candidates.Pure bulk silicon: Si peak at 519.9 cmвЃ»В№ dominates, but additional peaks at 1579.9 cmвЃ»В№ (G band) and 2670.2 cmвЃ»В№ (2D band) are incompatible with pure Si; bulk Si has no Raman features in those regions.
eliminated_candidates.MoS2 or other TMDs: Expected A1g/E2g modes near 380вЂ“410 cmвЃ»В№ are absent; region_fraction 300вЂ“500 is only 0.072, consistent with the weak Si second-order feature at 300.8 cmвЃ»В№, not TMD phonon modes.
eliminated_candidates.Highly defective graphene (D-band domina: D band near 1341.9 cmвЃ»В№ has very low rel_height (0.113) and very broad FWHM (144 cmвЃ»В№) suggesting it is a weak, broad background feature rather than a true sharp D peak; G band at 1579.9 cmвЃ»В№ is sharp (FWHM 17 cmвЃ»В№), inconsistent with highly disordered carbon.
eliminated_candidates.Multilayer graphene (>5 layers) / HOPG: 2D peak at 2670.2 cmвЃ»В№ has FWHM of 32.2 cmвЃ»В№, which is narrower than HOPG (~60вЂ“80 cmвЃ»В№) and consistent with few-layer graphene; I(2D)/I(G) ratio ~1.64 also points away from bulk graphite.
eliminated_candidates.Amorphous carbon: Sharp G peak (FWHM 17 cmвЃ»В№) and well-resolved 2D peak argue against amorphous carbon, which would show broad, heavily D-dominated spectrum.
eliminated_candidates.SiO2 or other oxides: No broad luminescence background or characteristic SiвЂ“O stretching modes; spectrum is dominated by crystalline Si phonon peak.

key_evidence: Dominant Si first-order phonon peak at 519.9 cmвЃ»В№, rel_height 25.87, FWHM 17.1 cmвЃ»В№ вЂ” sharp, symmetric, classic crystalline Si signature; this is the substrate.
G band at 1579.9 cmвЃ»В№, rel_height 2.70, FWHM 17.0 cmвЃ»В№ вЂ” narrow, sp2 carbon G mode consistent with ordered graphene/graphitic carbon.

2D (G') band at 2670.2 cmвЃ»В№, rel_height 4.41, FWHM 32.2 cmвЃ»В№ вЂ” single Lorentzian-like 2D band; FWHM ~32 cmвЃ»В№ is characteristic of bilayer or few-layer graphene (monolayer ~25 cmвЃ»В№, HOPG ~80 cmвЃ»В№).
I(2D)/I(G) relative height ratio в‰€ 4.41/2.70 = 1.63, consistent with few-layer graphene (1вЂ“3 layers) rather than bulk graphite (<1) or monolayer (>2).
region_fraction 500вЂ“900 = 0.497, dominated by the Si peak; region_fraction 2650вЂ“2750 = 0.084, confirming significant 2D band intensity; region_fraction 1550вЂ“1620 = 0.037, confirming G band.

Broad, weak feature centred near 1341.9 cmвЃ»В№ (FWHM 144 cmвЃ»В№, rel_height 0.113) вЂ” may represent a very weak D band or second-order Si combination band; D/G intensity ratio is very low, indicating low defect density in the carbon film.
Peak at 300.8 cmвЃ»В№ (rel_height 2.16) and 618.1 cmвЃ»В№ (rel_height 1.61) correspond to Si second-order 2TA(X) and 2TO(О“) combination/overtone modes, confirming crystalline Si substrate.
Peak at 971.8 cmвЃ»В№ (rel_height 3.32, FWHM 41.1 cmвЃ»В№) matches Si third-order/combination phonon features (~970 cmвЃ»В№, 2Г—TO), further confirming Si substrate.
Peak at 818.4 cmвЃ»В№ (rel_height 0.116) consistent with Si second-order W2 combination mode (~820 cmвЃ»В№).
Numerous weak peaks above 3000 cmвЃ»В№ (up to 6039 cmвЃ»В№) are consistent with higher-order Si phonon overtones and combination bands, common in high-SNR Si substrate spectra.
reasoning: STAGE 1 вЂ” DOMINANT FEATURES: The five highest-prominence peaks are: (1) 519.9 cmвЃ»В№ (prominence 25.87, FWHM 17.1 cmвЃ»В№) вЂ” unambiguously the first-order TO phonon of crystalline silicon; (2) 2670.2 cmвЃ»В№ (prominence 4.41, FWHM 32.2 cmвЃ»В№) вЂ” the graphene 2D band; (3) 971.8 cmвЃ»В№ (prominence 3.32, FWHM 41.1 cmвЃ»В№) вЂ” Si second/third-order phonon; (4) 1579.9 cmвЃ»В№ (prominence 2.70, FWHM 17.0 cmвЃ»В№) вЂ” carbon G band; (5) 300.8 cmвЃ»В№ (prominence 1.37) вЂ” Si 2TA overtone. The Si substrate dominates in absolute intensity. STAGE 2 вЂ” SECONDARY FEATURES: The carbon G band at 1579.9 cmвЃ»В№ is sharp (17 cmвЃ»В№), ruling out amorphous carbon. The 2D band at 2670.2 cmвЃ»В№ with FWHM ~32 cmвЃ»В№ is a key graphene fingerprint. The D band region (1341.9 cmвЃ»В№) shows only a very weak, broad feature (FWHM 144 cmвЃ»В№), indicating the carbon film is relatively defect-free. The 2D/G height ratio of ~1.63 is diagnostic for few-layer (bilayer or trilayer) graphene. Secondary Si overtones at 618.1, 818.4, 971.8, and a series of weak features extending to 6000+ cmвЃ»В№ all confirm a high-quality crystalline Si substrate. The weak feature at 1853.5 cmвЃ»В№ could be a Si combination band (TO+TA). STAGE 3 вЂ” SPECTRAL WEIGHT: The 500вЂ“900 cmвЃ»В№ region contains 49.7% of integrated intensity, consistent with the dominant Si peak. The 2650вЂ“2750 cmвЃ»В№ region holds 8.4% вЂ” substantial for a 2D band, indicating a non-negligible graphene overlayer. The 1550вЂ“1620 cmвЃ»В№ window holds 3.7%, confirming the G band. The 1300вЂ“1400 cmвЃ»В№ window holds only 0.4%, consistent with a very weak or absent D band. STAGE 4 вЂ” CANDIDATE EVALUATION: (a) Si substrate alone: ruled out because G (1579.9) and 2D (2670.2) peaks have no origin in Si. (b) Graphene/few-layer graphene on Si: fully consistent вЂ” Si peak at 519.9, G at 1579.9 with FWHM 17 cmвЃ»В№, 2D at 2670.2 with FWHM ~32 cmвЃ»В№, 2D/G ~1.6, weak D band в†’ few-layer graphene (bilayer most consistent with FWHM and 2D/G ratio). (c) HOPG or bulk graphite: 2D FWHM would be ~60вЂ“80 cmвЃ»В№ and 2D/G < 1; both inconsistent with data. (d) TMDs: no phonon modes in 350вЂ“420 cmвЃ»В№ window, ruled out. (e) Amorphous carbon: G peak too narrow, 2D too well-defined. STAGE 5 вЂ” DECISION: The spectrum is that of few-layer graphene (likely bilayer or trilayer, given 2D FWHM ~32 cmвЃ»В№ and 2D/G ~1.6) on a crystalline silicon substrate. The SNR is excellent (153), confirming the spectral features are real. Confidence 0.82 reflects minor ambiguity in exact layer count and uncertainty about whether the broad 1341 cmвЃ»В№ feature is truly a D band or a Si combination artifact.
